title: Xtra Sweet Irish Eggplant Casserole
description: A sweet and aerated Irish dish featuring eggplant, this casserole is a crowd-pleaser and gluten-free friendly. It is made with kneaded ingredients and baked to perfection.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, cook the ground beef over medium-high heat until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the chopped onion and minced garlic to the skillet and cook until the onion is translucent.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked ground beef mixture, tomato sauce, and shredded cheese.
In a separate bowl, knead together the gluten-free oats and brown sugar.
In a greased 9x13 inch baking dish, create a layer of eggplant slices.
Spread half of the ground beef mixture over the eggplant layer.
Sprinkle half of the oat mixture over the ground beef layer.
Repeat the layers, starting with the eggplant, then the ground beef mixture, and finally the oat mixture.
Bake the casserole in the preheated oven for 45 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the casserole is hot and bubbly.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
